did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper uses a hybrid classifier combining Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and Support Vector Machines (SVM) to classify melanoma as benign or malignant and detect birthmark moles from clinical/dermoscopy images. Specifically, an ANN is used to detect if a lesion is a birthmark, and an SVM is used to classify non-birthmark lesions as malignant or benign. The system achieved an accuracy of 91.2%.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper presents an original automated diagnosis system for melanoma classification and birthmark detection using a hybrid classifier (ANN and SVM) on clinical/dermoscopy images. It details a proposed scheme including preprocessing, feature extraction (color, texture, border), and classifier design, and reports experimental results with 91.2% accuracy. Although published in a conference proceedings (ViTECoN), it contains original experimental work rather than summarizing prior literature.

title: Melanoma Classification and Birthmark Mole Detection on Clinical Images
journal: 2019 International Conference on Vision Towards Emerging Trends in Communication and Networking (ViTECoN)
brief_summary: This study proposes an automated diagnosis system for classifying melanoma as benign or malignant and detecting birthmark moles from clinical and dermoscopy images. The method utilizes a hybrid classifier combining Artificial Neural Networks (ANN) and Support Vector Machines (SVM), where the ANN identifies birthmarks and the SVM classifies non-birthmark lesions. Feature extraction focuses on color, texture, and border characteristics, including specific metrics for concavity and lesion area division, to handle both complete and incomplete lesions. The system was evaluated using a confusion matrix to assess its performance in distinguishing between skin cancer types and birthmarks. The proposed scheme achieved a classification accuracy of 91.2%, demonstrating its effectiveness in providing early detection with a smaller number of features.

lesion_types_studied.details: The primary target for classification into benign or malignant categories. The system distinguishes between benign and malignant melanoma.
A specific class of benign lesions detected by the ANN component to distinguish from cancerous lesions. The system identifies if a lesion is a birthmark and stops processing if so.
Non-cancerous melanoma lesions classified by the SVM component. The system classifies non-birthmark lesions as either benign or malignant.
Cancerous melanoma lesions classified by the SVM component. An alert is generated if the lesion is identified as malignant.
Mentioned in the Related Work section regarding the MED-NODE system [1] which distinguishes melanoma from cellular naevi. Not used in the proposed system's experiments.

Listed in the Related Work section describing the work of Reshma M et al. [10] which recognizes various melanoma types. These specific subtypes were not the focus of the proposed system's binary classification task.

all_task_types.target: Binary classification of skin lesions as malignant or benign, and detection/classification of birthmark moles.
all_task_types.details: The paper proposes a hybrid classifier (ANN and SVM) to classify melanoma as benign or malignant and detect birthmark moles. The ANN component detects if a lesion is a birthmark (binary classification/detection), and the SVM component classifies non-birthmark lesions as malignant or benign (binary classification). The system achieved an accuracy of 91.2%.

datasets_used.name: Clinical/Dermoscopy Images (Unspecified Collection)
datasets_used.description: The study utilized clinical and dermoscopy images of skin lesions to develop and evaluate an automated diagnosis system. The specific source, name, or public/private status of the dataset is not reported in the text.

use.training: Used to train the hybrid classifier (ANN and SVM) for detecting birthmarks and classifying melanoma as benign or malignant.
use.testing: Used to evaluate the system's performance, achieving an accuracy of 91.2%.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report any formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance was evaluated using accuracy (91.2%) and a confusion matrix.

Clinical/Dermoscopy Images (Unspecified.notes: The system achieved an accuracy of 91.2% in detecting skin cancer, birthmark mole, and classifying melanoma as benign or malignant. The confusion matrix of the ANN classifier is provided in Fig. 4, but specific values are not reported in the text.

architecture_used.main_model: Hybrid classifier combining Artificial Neural Network (ANN) and Support Vector Machine (SVM)

architecture_details.ANN_role: Used to detect if a lesion is a birthmark (binary classification/detection). If a birthmark is detected, the program stops.
architecture_details.SVM_role: Used to classify non-birthmark lesions as malignant or benign.
architecture_details.ANN_description: A program aimed at modelling the human brain to solve complex problems, consisting of many neurons working together.
architecture_details.SVM_description: A supervised learning technique used for classification and regression analysis, noted for providing better results for two classes.

training_details.notes: Specific training parameters, hyperparameters, or optimization details for the ANN and SVM are not reported in the text.

classification_or_output_head.output_type: Binary classification (Birthmark vs. Non-Birthmark for ANN; Malignant vs. Benign for SVM)
classification_or_output_head.alert_mechanism: An alert is generated if the lesion is identified as malignant.

preprocessing_techniques: Hair removal using partial differential equation (PDE)
Feature dimensionality reduction using Principal Components Analysis (PCA)
conclusions: The proposed automated diagnosis system successfully detects skin cancer, identifies birthmark moles, and classifies melanoma as benign or malignant with an accuracy of 91.2%.

The system utilizes a hybrid classifier combining an Artificial Neural Network (ANN) for birthmark detection and a Support Vector Machine (SVM) for classifying non-birthmark lesions as benign or malignant.
The method effectively classifies lesions using a smaller number of features (color, texture, and border) compared to earlier methods that required more features.
The inclusion of border features, specifically concavity characteristics, improves classification results, addressing limitations in previous methods that did not concentrate on border features.
The proposed scheme is robust and effective for both complete and incomplete lesions.
The system generates an alert if a malignant lesion is detected, while stopping processing if a birthmark is identified.
Future work aims to predict the probability of a birthmark mole turning into cancer to further improve the accuracy and efficiency of the detection system.
important_remarks: The specific dataset source, size, and class distribution are not reported; the study uses an unspecified collection of clinical and dermoscopy images.
No external validation was performed; the system was evaluated only on the internal dataset.
Formal statistical significance testing (e.g., p-values, confidence intervals) is absent; performance is reported solely as 91.2% accuracy.
The confusion matrix for the ANN classifier is referenced as Figure 4 but specific values are not provided in the text.
Training details for the ANN and SVM models, including hyperparameters, optimization methods, and loss functions, are not reported.
The paper does not address potential data leakage risks or provide details on train/validation/test splits.
The system is designed to stop processing if a birthmark is detected, which may limit its utility in comprehensive lesion analysis workflows.

The study focuses on a hybrid classifier using traditional features (color, texture, border) rather than deep learning architectures, which may limit generalizability compared to modern CNN-based methods.
